pull.title: net_processing: fix BIP152 first integer interpretation
pull.body:
Fixes #35542

According to the BIP152, the first integer in `sendcmpct` message shall be interpreted as a boolean (and MUST have a value of either 1 or 0). We currently correctly interpret it as boolean, however, we accept any value >=1 and treat it as `true`, deviating from the specification. This PR fixes it.

events.message: net_processing: fix BIP152 first integer interpretation

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/35550. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | ACK | [edilmedeiros](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35550#issuecomment-4741967540), [davidgumberg](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35550#issuecomment-4746988699), [w0xlt](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35550#pullrequestreview-4530497404), [Sjors](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35550#issuecomment-4751481264) | | Concept ACK | [yancyribbens](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35550#issuecomment-4736418067) | | Stale ACK | [jonatack](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35550#pullrequestreview-4519303864) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore.
ACK 2e6276bcb3560bf07f6daa689a052200a4eb0a54, might be good to verify that other implementations would not be affected by this tightening. Sanity checked getpeerinfo and -netinfo output.

events.message: test: announce field must be 0 or 1 in sendcmpct

events.body:
utACK abc33ff043f07226d1f1f88a1d1ddce8dccdd930

> might be good to verify that other implementations would not be affected by this tightening.

This was found during the review of the draft implementation for btcd. Floresta doesn't implement bip152.
crACK https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/abc33ff043f07226d1f1f88a1d1ddce8dccdd930 Seems reasonable to comply with BIP152 strictly, test looks good as well.

ACK abc33ff043f07226d1f1f88a1d1ddce8dccdd930

@edilmedeiros wrote:

> This was found during the review of the draft implementation for btcd.

Glad to see more adoption.

The other independent implementations (not code forks) that implement BIP152 support (usually just the message encoding), as far as I'm aware, seem fine: 
- bcoin: https://github.com/bcoin-org/bcoin/blob/v2.2.0/lib/net/pool.js#L3774-L3776 (blockMode is a number, not forced a bool, but it defaults to 0 and afaik is never changed)
- gocoin: https://github.com/piotrnar/gocoin/blob/1.11.0/client/network/tick.go#L813-L814
- libbitcoin: https://github.com/libbitcoin/libbitcoin-network/blob/master/include/bitcoin/network/messages/peer/detail/send_compact.hpp#L52 / https://github.com/libbitcoin/libbitcoin-network/blob/master/src/messages/peer/detail/send_compact.cpp#L91 (not sure if it ever actually sends the message, but encodes it correctly).
Happy to run this for a few days on a node and see if anything that connects to me triggers the misbehavior.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -263,6 +263,17 @@ def check_announcement_of_new_block(node, peer, predicate):
         test_node.send_and_ping(msg_sendcmpct(announce=False, version=2))
         check_announcement_of_new_block(node, test_node, lambda p: "cmpctblock" not in p.last_message and "headers" in p.last_message)
 
+    # BIP152 mandates that the announce field of a sendcmpct message is a
+    # boolean and MUST have a value of either 1 or 0. Sending any other value
+    # should be treated as misbehavior and lead to a disconnect.
+    def test_invalid_sendcmpct_announce(self):
+        node = self.nodes[0]
+        bad_peer = node.add_p2p_connection(TestP2PConn())
+        msg = msg_sendcmpct(announce=2, version=2)
+        with node.assert_debug_log(['invalid sendcmpct announce field']):
+            bad_peer.send_without_ping(msg)
+            bad_peer.wait_for_disconnect()
comments.body:
nit, can probably use the helper

```diff
@@ -271,8 +271,7 @@ class CompactBlocksTest(BitcoinTestFramework):
         bad_peer = node.add_p2p_connection(TestP2PConn())
         msg = msg_sendcmpct(announce=2, version=2)
         with node.assert_debug_log(['invalid sendcmpct announce field']):
-            bad_peer.send_without_ping(msg)
-            bad_peer.wait_for_disconnect()
+            bad_peer.send_await_disconnect(msg)
```
Musing, perhaps "byte" or "message" instead of "field" here but no strong opinion.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -263,6 +263,17 @@ def check_announcement_of_new_block(node, peer, predicate):
         test_node.send_and_ping(msg_sendcmpct(announce=False, version=2))
         check_announcement_of_new_block(node, test_node, lambda p: "cmpctblock" not in p.last_message and "headers" in p.last_message)
 
+    # BIP152 mandates that the announce field of a sendcmpct message is a
+    # boolean and MUST have a value of either 1 or 0. Sending any other value
+    # should be treated as misbehavior and lead to a disconnect.
+    def test_invalid_sendcmpct_announce(self):
+        node = self.nodes[0]
+        bad_peer = node.add_p2p_connection(TestP2PConn())
+        msg = msg_sendcmpct(announce=2, version=2)
+        with node.assert_debug_log(['invalid sendcmpct announce field']):
+            bad_peer.send_without_ping(msg)
+            bad_peer.wait_for_disconnect()

comments.body: I'm fine with "byte" or "field", no strong opinion on this. Will keep as is for now. Thanks.
